meta.title: Opten — AI Prompt Scorer
hero.title: Stop wasting credits on bad prompts
hero.subtitle1: Opten evaluates your prompt for the specific AI model, shows what's wrong, and fixes it in one click.
partners.label: After installing, use it on these sites:
steps.01.title: Write your prompt as usual
steps.01.desc: Opten embeds directly into the generator interface. No copy-pasting to other windows.
steps.02.desc: A color ring, specific recommendations, and a diagnosis: what's wrong and why. For your specific model, not just "in general".
steps.03.desc: "Enhance" rewrites the prompt following all rules of the chosen AI model. Paste and generate.
features.card1.title: 43 models. Each with its own rules.
features.card1.desc: Midjourney loves short metaphors. Flux requires technical parameters. DALL·E wants detailed descriptions. Opten knows the rules of every model — and checks your prompt against them, not "in general".
features.card2.title: Works where you generate
features.card2.desc:
No need to switch tabs or copy text.
Opten appears right inside your generator interface — like Grammarly appears in Gmail.
features.card3.title: Every bad prompt burns your credits
features.card3.desc: Opten users save 30%–50% of generation credits. With an average spend of $20–50/mo on generators, Opten pays for itself in the first hour of use.
features.card4.title: Prompt improvement in one click
features.card4.desc:
"Enhance" rewrites the prompt in 2 seconds.
It considers style, technical parameters, model constraints, and your original intent.
privacy.item1.title: We don't store prompts
privacy.item1.desc: The extension analyzes text locally and via a secure API channel. Your prompts and images are never saved on our servers.
privacy.item2.title: We don't sell or share data
privacy.item2.desc: We don't sell user content, use it for advertising, or provide it to third parties for model training.
privacy.item3.title: Minimal permissions
privacy.item3.desc: Opten requests only the browser permissions necessary for operation. No access to your history, bookmarks, or other tabs.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.body:
This document constitutes a public offer to enter into a contract for access to the Opten service ("Service").

1. Parties to the Agreement

Depending on the User's region and payment method, the Seller under this Agreement is one of the following entities:

1.1. For Users paying via YuKassa (Russia and CIS)

Individual Entrepreneur Voronezhtsev Vladislav Pavlovich
INN (Tax ID): 723016676391
Address: 625062, Tyumen, Dmitry Mendeleev St., 2, bldg. 1, apt./office 148, Russia
Email: zignifer@gmail.com

Pursuant to Article 437 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation, this document constitutes a public offer. Acceptance of the offer is deemed to occur upon the actions specified in Section 3.2.

1.2. For Users paying via Paddle (all other countries)

Individual Entrepreneur Nikolai Shupletsov (registered in Georgia)
Tax ID: 306389672
Address: 5 Samgori Street, Tbilisi, Georgia, 0190
Email: ummohwell@gmail.com

For international payments, Paddle.com Market Limited ("Paddle") acts as the Merchant of Record. This means that when paying via Paddle, the User is purchasing access to the Service from Paddle as an authorized reseller of IE Nikolai Shupletsov's product. Purchase terms are also governed by Paddle's Buyer Terms and Conditions.

2. Definitions

  • Service — the "Opten" software, distributed as a Google Chrome browser extension, designed to analyze and improve prompts for AI image and video generators.
  • Seller — the legal entity determined in accordance with Section 1 of this Agreement.
  • Customer (User) — an individual at least 18 years old who has accepted the terms of this Agreement.
  • Subscription — paid access to the extended functionality of the Service ("Pro" plan) with automatic renewal every 30 calendar days.
  • One-Time Access — paid access to the extended functionality of the Service ("Pro" plan) for 30 calendar days without automatic renewal.
  • Billing Period — 30 (thirty) calendar days from the moment of activation or renewal of a subscription (or One-Time Access).

3. Subject of the Offer

3.1. The Seller agrees to provide the Customer with access to the functions of the Service in accordance with the selected plan, and the Customer agrees to pay for the services on the terms set out in this Agreement.

3.2. Acceptance of this offer constitutes any of the following actions:

  • Installing the Opten extension from the Chrome Web Store
  • Creating an account in the Service
  • Paying for a subscription or One-Time Access

3.3. The date of contract conclusion is the date of acceptance.

3.4. By registering, the User confirms their consent to the processing of personal data in accordance with the Privacy Policy.

4. Plans and Pricing

4.1. Free Plan

  • Free extension installation and account registration
  • Access to paid Service features (including prompt scoring and enhancement) is not included in this plan
  • Price: 0 ₽ / $0

4.2. "Pro" Plan (Subscription)

  • 300 prompt checks per billing period
  • One-click prompt enhancement (Auto-enhance)
  • Priority analysis speed
  • Early access to new models
  • Support in Telegram
  • Automatic renewal every 30 days
  • Price for RU/CIS users: 199 (one hundred ninety-nine) rubles per billing period
  • Price for international users: equivalent in USD/EUR as shown on opten.space at the time of payment (inclusive of applicable taxes calculated by Paddle)

4.3. "Pro" Plan (One-Time Access)

  • 300 prompt checks per billing period
  • One-click prompt enhancement (Auto-enhance)
  • Support in Telegram
  • Access provided for 30 calendar days from the date of payment
  • Automatic renewal is not performed
  • Price for RU/CIS users: 299 (two hundred ninety-nine) rubles (one-time)
  • Price for international users: equivalent in USD/EUR as shown on opten.space at the time of payment

4.4. The Seller may change prices, provided the Customer is notified at least 14 calendar days before the change takes effect. Price changes do not affect the current paid period.

5. Payment Terms

5.1. Payment is made by non-cash methods through the respective payment provider:

  • YuKassa (LLC NCO "YuMoney", licensed by the Bank of Russia) — for users in Russia and CIS. Supported payment methods: bank card, Faster Payment System (SBP), YuMoney.
  • Paddle (Paddle.com Market Limited) — for international users. Paddle acts as the Merchant of Record and handles all applicable taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax).

5.2. Subscription (Auto-Renewal)

5.2.1. The subscription renews automatically every 30 calendar days. Funds are charged on the date corresponding to the first payment date. Automatic renewal requires the payment method to be saved.

5.2.2. The Customer may cancel automatic renewal at any time via:
- The extension interface (popup → "Cancel subscription")
- The user dashboard on opten.space
- The Paddle customer portal (for international users)

5.2.3. Upon cancellation, access to "Pro" features is retained until the end of the current paid period. The saved payment method is unlinked.

5.2.4. If automatic renewal fails, the Seller may retry the charge. If the payment cannot be collected within 7 calendar days, access to "Pro" features is terminated and the Customer is moved to the Free plan.

5.3. One-Time Access

5.3.1. With One-Time Access, payment is made once. The payment method is not saved and no automatic charges occur.

5.3.2. Access to "Pro" features is valid for 30 calendar days from the date of payment. After this period expires, the Customer is automatically moved to the Free plan.

5.3.3. To continue using "Pro" features after the One-Time Access expires, the Customer may purchase a new One-Time Access or a Subscription.

6. Service Provision

6.1. The Service is available 24/7. The Seller makes reasonable efforts to ensure uninterrupted operation but does not guarantee the absence of technical downtime.

6.2. The Seller may temporarily suspend the Service for maintenance, notifying the Customer when possible.

6.3. Prompt analysis results are generated by AI and are advisory only. The Seller does not guarantee specific image or video generation outcomes.

7. Rights and Obligations

7.1. The Seller shall:

  • Provide access to the Service in accordance with the selected plan
  • Protect personal data in accordance with the Privacy Policy
  • Notify the Customer of material changes to service terms

7.2. The Seller may:

  • Modify the functionality of the Service without prior notice
  • Suspend or terminate the Customer's access if this Agreement is violated
  • Set usage limits for each plan

7.3. The Customer shall:

  • Not use the Service for purposes contrary to applicable law
  • Not attempt to circumvent the Service's technical limitations
  • Not distribute harmful content via the Service
  • Not use the Service to process prompts containing illegal, offensive, discriminatory content, or content that infringes third-party rights
  • Provide accurate information upon registration
  • Not share account credentials with third parties

8. Intellectual Property

8.1. All rights to the Service, including source code, design, logos, and documentation, belong to the Seller.

8.2. The Customer receives a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Service as intended.

8.3. The Customer retains all rights to prompts they submit. By submitting prompts to the Service, the Customer grants the Seller a limited license to process them solely for the purpose of providing the Service.

9. Limitation of Liability

9.1. The Service is provided "as is". The Seller is not responsible for the Service's suitability for specific purposes or the Customer's expectations.

9.2. The Seller is not liable for damages arising from the use or inability to use the Service, including lost profits.

9.3. The Seller's total liability is limited to the amount paid by the Customer for the current billing period.

10. Term and Termination

10.1. This Agreement is effective from the moment of acceptance until terminated by either party.

10.2. The Customer may terminate this Agreement at any time by canceling the subscription (if applicable) and removing the extension. Refunds are governed by the Refund Policy.

10.3. The Seller may unilaterally terminate this Agreement if the Customer violates these terms, by notifying the Customer via email.

11. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law

11.1. For contracts with IE Voronezhtsev V. P. (payments via YuKassa)

All disputes shall be resolved through negotiations. A mandatory claim procedure applies, with a response period of 15 business days. If a dispute cannot be resolved through negotiations, it shall be subject to the courts at the Seller's location in accordance with the laws of the Russian Federation.

11.2. For contracts with IE Nikolai Shupletsov (payments via Paddle)

This Agreement is governed by the laws of Georgia, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. All disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Tbilisi, Georgia, except where mandatory consumer protection laws of the Customer's country of residence provide otherwise.

12. Seller Details

12.1. IE Voronezhtsev Vladislav Pavlovich (Russia)

INN (Tax ID): 723016676391
Address: 625062, Tyumen, Dmitry Mendeleev St., 2, bldg. 1, apt./office 148
Bank: Ekaterinburg Branch of JSC "Alfa-Bank"
Account: 40802810638390002456
Correspondent Account: 30101810100000000964
BIC: 046577964
Email: zignifer@gmail.com
Phone: +7 (929) 262-11-17
Website: opten.space

12.2. Individual Entrepreneur Nikolai Shupletsov (Georgia)

Tax ID: 306389672
Address: 5 Samgori Street, Tbilisi, Georgia, 0190
Email: ummohwell@gmail.com
Phone: +995 599 15 44 32
Website: opten.space
privacy.body:
1. General Provisions

This Privacy Policy ("Policy") describes how personal data of users of the Opten service ("Service") is collected, used, and protected.

The Opten Service is operated by two legal entities depending on the user's region and payment method. Both Operators apply the unified principles of personal data processing described in this Policy.

1.1. Operator for users in Russia and CIS countries (payments via YuKassa)

Individual Entrepreneur Vladislav Voronezhtsev
INN (Tax ID): 723016676391
Address: 625062, Tyumen, Dmitry Mendeleev St., 2, bldg. 1, apt./office 148, Russia
Email: zignifer@gmail.com
Phone: +7 (929) 262-11-17

1.2. Operator for users in all other countries (payments via Paddle)

Individual Entrepreneur Nikolai Shupletsov (registered in Georgia)
Tax ID: 306389672
Address: 5 Samgori Street, Tbilisi, Georgia, 0190
Email: ummohwell@gmail.com
Phone: +995 599 15 44 32

1.3. Determining the Operator for a Specific User

Prior to purchase, the data Controller is IE Voronezhtsev V. P. (for users accessing from Russia, Belarus, and CIS countries) or IE Nikolai Shupletsov (for users from other countries). Upon payment, the Operator becomes the legal entity that received the payment through the corresponding payment provider:

  • YuKassa — for payments in Russian rubles (Operator: IE Voronezhtsev V. P.)
  • Paddle — for international payments (Operator: IE Nikolai Shupletsov)

By using the Service, the User agrees to the terms of this Policy. If the User does not agree with the Policy, they must discontinue use of the Service.

2. Information We Collect

2.1. Information Provided by the User

  • Email address — during registration and authentication in the Service
  • Payment details — when subscribing or making a one-time purchase. The data is processed by the corresponding payment provider (YuKassa or Paddle) and is not stored by the Operator. The Operator does not receive or store full bank card details.

2.2. Information Collected Automatically

  • User identifier (UUID) — for usage limit tracking
  • Number of checks performed — for tier limit enforcement
  • Browser type and version — for compatibility
  • IP address — to determine region and payment currency

2.3. Information We Do NOT Collect or Store

  • Prompt text — not stored on Operator servers. Prompts are transmitted through a secure API channel for analysis and are not logged.
  • Generated images and videos — generation results are not uploaded or processed by the Service.
  • Browser history, bookmarks, data from other tabs.
  • Geolocation data.

2.4. Reference Images

When using the prompt analysis feature, the Service may automatically extract reference images (style, character, and other reference images) uploaded by the User in the AI generator interface. These images are transmitted through a secure API channel solely for analyzing prompt quality in the context of attached materials. Reference images are not stored on Operator servers and are not used for any other purposes.

2.5. Chrome Web Store User Data Policy Compliance

The Operator's use of information received from Google APIs and through Chrome adheres to the Chrome Web Store User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements. The Operator does not transfer user data to third parties, except for the purposes described in Section 5 of this Policy, and does not use it for personalized advertising, creditworthiness determination, or sale.

2.6. Sites Accessed by the Extension

The Opten extension injects its prompt-scoring interface directly into the pages of supported AI generator websites. To do so, the extension requests host permissions for the following domains. These permissions are granted once by the User when the extension is installed or updated through the Chrome Web Store:

  • syntx.ai — SYNTX AI image and video generator
  • higgsfield.ai — Higgsfield AI generator (images, video, audio)
  • images.higgs.ai — Higgsfield CDN used to serve reference images uploaded by the User into the generator interface
  • www.freepik.com — Freepik Pikaso interface (AI image and video generation)
  • pikaso.cdnpk.net — Freepik Pikaso CDN for reference images

Within these permissions the extension reads only the prompt text in the AI generator's editor and the reference images the User has voluntarily uploaded into the generator interface (see Sections 2.3 and 2.4). Browsing history, cookies, content of other tabs and any other on-page data are never read or transmitted by the extension.

The User may revoke these permissions at any time via chrome://extensions → "Opten" → "Site access". Doing so stops the extension from functioning on the corresponding site but does not affect the User's account or any active paid subscription.

3. Purposes of Data Processing

The Operator processes personal data exclusively for the following purposes:

  • Providing access to the Service and its features
  • User identification and usage limit tracking
  • Payment processing and subscription management
  • Sending service notifications (subscription status, Service changes)
  • Service quality improvement
  • Fulfilling obligations under the Public Offer Agreement
  • Compliance with applicable law

4. Legal Bases for Processing

For Operator IE Voronezhtsev V. P. (Russia):

  • User consent (Art. 6(1)(1) of Federal Law No. 152-FZ "On Personal Data")
  • Performance of a contract to which the data subject is a party (Art. 6(1)(5) of FZ-152)

For Operator IE Nikolai Shupletsov (Georgia) and for users in the EU/UK:

  • Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) — to provide access to the Service
  • Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) — to improve the Service and prevent abuse
  • Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) — where applicable
  • Legal bases under the Law of Georgia on Personal Data Protection

5. Sharing Data with Third Parties

The Operator does not sell, rent, or transfer personal data to third parties, except to the following categories of recipients:

  • YuKassa (LLC NCO "YuMoney") — for payment processing for users in Russia and CIS. YuKassa is a certified payment data operator and processes card data in accordance with PCI DSS.
  • Paddle.com Market Limited — for international payment processing. Paddle acts as the Merchant of Record for international transactions and is independently responsible for PCI DSS compliance, tax handling, refunds, and chargebacks. Paddle's privacy policy: https://www.paddle.com/legal/privacy
  • Supabase Inc. — for storing user accounts and subscription data. Data is stored on servers in Europe.
  • Anthropic, PBC — for prompt and reference image analysis via the Claude API. Data is transmitted in real time and is not retained by the provider for model training.
  • Cloudflare, Inc. — for routing API requests via Cloudflare Workers. Cloudflare acts as an intermediate server (proxy). Data is transmitted in transit and not stored.
  • Google LLC — for OAuth 2.0 authentication. Google receives data necessary to verify the User's identity (email address). Data processing is governed by Google's Privacy Policy.
  • Legal requirements — as required by the laws of the Russian Federation, Georgia, or other applicable jurisdictions.

6. International Data Transfers

Because we use service providers located outside Russia and Georgia (including in the United States, European Union, and United Kingdom), your personal data may be transferred internationally. When such transfers occur, appropriate safeguards apply, including Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission or the providers' own adequacy mechanisms.

7. Data Retention and Security

Personal data is retained for the duration of the user's account. Upon account deletion, data is removed within 30 calendar days. Payment data is retained by the payment providers (YuKassa and Paddle) in accordance with their policies and applicable tax law (typically 5–10 years).

The Operator implements reasonable organizational and technical measures to protect personal data from unauthorized or accidental access, destruction, modification, blocking, copying, distribution, and other unlawful actions:

  • Data encryption in transit (TLS/HTTPS)
  • Authentication via secure tokens (JWT)
  • Access control
  • Regular security audits

8. User Rights

You have the following rights:

  • Access — request information about your personal data held by the Operator
  • Rectification — request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data
  • Erasure — request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
  • Restriction — request that we limit processing of your data
  • Portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format (for EU/UK users)
  • Object — object to processing based on legitimate interests (for EU/UK users)
  • Withdraw consent — where processing is based on consent, withdraw it at any time
  • Delete account — delete your account and all associated data
  • Lodge a complaint with Roskomnadzor (for Russian users) or with the appropriate supervisory authority in your country

To exercise these rights, contact the relevant Operator (see Section 1). Requests are processed within 30 calendar days in accordance with FZ-152 and GDPR requirements.

9. Cookies and Analytics

The opten.space website uses cookies for:
- Essential cookies — authentication, session management, security (mandatory)
- Analytics cookies — Google Analytics 4 for user behavior analysis (with consent)

The Opten extension uses browser storage (chrome.storage) exclusively for storing user preferences and authentication data.

Users can control cookies through browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may cause the Service to malfunction.

10. Children's Privacy

The Service is not intended for individuals under 18 years old. The Operator does not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18. If you become aware that a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us and we will delete the data.

11. Changes to the Policy

The Operator may update this Policy from time to time. The current version is always published at opten.space/privacy. Continued use of the Service after changes are made indicates the User's acceptance of the updated Policy.

12. Contact Information

For privacy-related inquiries:

Users in Russia and CIS:
IE Voronezhtsev Vladislav Pavlovich
Email: zignifer@gmail.com
Phone: +7 (929) 262-11-17

Users in other countries:
IE Nikolai Shupletsov
Email: ummohwell@gmail.com
Phone: +995 599 15 44 32

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.body:
This Refund Policy ("Policy") sets out the conditions and procedure for refunds of subscriptions and One-Time Access purchases to the Opten service ("Service").

1. Who Processes Refunds

Depending on the payment provider used, refunds are processed by one of the following Sellers:

  • Payments via YuKassa (rubles) — Individual Entrepreneur Voronezhtsev Vladislav Pavlovich, INN 723016676391
  • Payments via Paddle (international currencies) — Individual Entrepreneur Nikolai Shupletsov, Tax ID 306389672. Refunds are technically issued by Paddle.com Market Limited, which acts as the Merchant of Record.

Refunds are issued through the same method and payment provider used for the original payment.

2. Canceling a Subscription and Ending Access

Subscription (auto-renewal):

2.1. The Customer may cancel their "Pro" subscription at any time via:
- The Opten extension interface (popup → "Cancel subscription" button)
- The user dashboard on opten.space
- The Paddle customer portal (for international users)

2.2. Upon cancellation:

  • Automatic renewal is stopped
  • The saved payment method (card) is unlinked
  • Access to "Pro" features is retained until the end of the current paid period (30 days from the last payment)
  • After the paid period ends, the Customer is automatically moved to the Free plan

2.3. Cancellation is not grounds for a refund of the current paid period.

One-Time Access:

2.4. With One-Time Access, no automatic renewal occurs and no cancellation is needed. Access to "Pro" features is valid for 30 calendar days from the date of payment.

2.5. After One-Time Access expires, the Customer is automatically moved to the Free plan. To continue using "Pro", a new payment must be made.

3. Refund Eligibility

3.1. Refunds are available in the following cases:

3.1.1. Refund within 3 days of payment

If no more than 3 (three) calendar days have passed since the payment (subscription or One-Time Access) and the Customer has not used the paid features of the Service (0 checks used during the paid period), a full refund is issued.

3.1.2. Technical issues

If the Service was unavailable for more than 72 consecutive hours during the paid period due to the Seller's fault, the Customer may request a refund proportional to the downtime period or an equivalent extension of access.

3.1.3. Erroneous charges

In case of double charges or other erroneous payments, the overpaid amount is refunded in full.

3.1.4. Unintended auto-renewal

If the Customer did not intend to continue the subscription but was charged for auto-renewal, and no more than 7 calendar days have passed since the charge, and the Customer has not used the paid features of the Service during the new billing period, the Seller may, at its sole discretion, issue a full or partial refund as a goodwill gesture.

4. When Refunds Are Not Issued

Refunds are not provided in the following cases:

  • The Customer has used the paid features of the Service and more than 3 days have passed since payment
  • The Customer violated the Terms of Service, resulting in account suspension
  • Service unavailability is caused by force majeure, issues on the User's side (incompatible browser, blockers, internet connection problems), or actions of third parties not associated with the Seller
  • Dissatisfaction with prompt scoring recommendations (analysis results are advisory and depend on the input provided)
  • Payment was made using a promo code or promotion specifically marked as "non-refundable"

5. How to Request a Refund

5.1. For payments via YuKassa (users in Russia and CIS)

Send a request to zignifer@gmail.com with subject "Refund Request" including:

  • The email address used to register with the Service
  • Date and amount of payment
  • Payment number (from the YuKassa receipt)
  • Payment type (subscription or One-Time Access)
  • Reason for the refund request

5.2. For payments via Paddle (international users)

Send a request to ummohwell@gmail.com with subject "Refund Request" including:

  • The email address used to register with the Service
  • Paddle order number (from the email receipt)
  • Date of payment
  • Reason for the refund request

Alternatively, you may contact Paddle directly at paddle.net by providing your email or transaction number to locate the order.

6. Processing Time

6.1. Requests are reviewed within 5 business days of receipt.

6.2. If approved, the refund is issued through the same method as the original payment:

  • YuKassa: refund to the bank card or sender's account (for SBP payments) within 5–10 business days (depending on the bank)
  • Paddle: refund to the bank card or other payment method used within 5–10 business days (depending on the bank and payment system). Confirmation of the refund comes from Paddle.

7. Chargebacks

If the Customer has concerns about any charge, please contact the Seller at the appropriate email address before initiating a chargeback with the bank. The Seller is committed to resolving issues quickly and fairly. Initiating a chargeback without first attempting to resolve the issue with the Seller may result in account suspension.

8. Contact Information

For users in Russia and CIS (payments via YuKassa):

IE Voronezhtsev Vladislav Pavlovich
Email: zignifer@gmail.com
Phone: +7 (929) 262-11-17
Response hours: Mon–Fri, 10:00–18:00 (MSK+2)

For international users (payments via Paddle):

IE Nikolai Shupletsov
Email: ummohwell@gmail.com
Phone: +995 599 15 44 32
Response hours: Mon–Fri, 10:00–18:00 (GMT+4)
